At what stage of your journey are you? (no shame if you're just getting started, we all got to start somewhere).

author: Devon Hoyt
text: What’s up everyone 👋 glad to be here.My name is Devon.1. From A to Z — how I got into self-improvement:Life kept handing me wake-up calls. Health scares, financial stress, family responsibilities, and realizing that nobody was coming to save me. I started learning out of necessity—how systems work, how habits work, how mindset works—because staying the same wasn’t an option anymore.2. The pain that pushed me:Feeling stuck while knowing I had people depending on me. That mix of frustration, fear, and responsibility is a powerful teacher. I didn’t want to just survive—I wanted to become someone my kids could look up to.3. Coaching / consulting path:Not a formal coach yet. I’m more in the “learn it, apply it, share it” phase. I help people by breaking things down simply, sharing what I’ve tested in real life, and being honest about what works and what doesn’t.4. How long I’ve been in self-improvement:Several years of trial, error, setbacks, and course corrections. Still learning every day.5. Current stage of my journey:Actively building. Still sharpening habits, tightening discipline, and leveling up consistency. Not finished—just committed.Looking forward to learning from everyone here and contributing where I can. 👊

author: Scott Northwolf
text: @Devon Hoyt epic, Devon! Thank you for sharing. Only when the pain of staying the same outweights the pain of facing the resistance and making a change is when we'll finally make a change. I heard from great men that the moment in which the truly became men was the very moment in which they realized nobody was coming to save them and they had to figure things out by themselves, it has been my experience as well.I'm really glad to have you here and looking forward to connect.

author: Andrea Piras
text: I got into self-improvement in the summer of 2022, almost 4 years ago. I did it because I had very few friends and life wasn't going as I wanted, so I decided to start meditating, journaling, exercising at home and various stuff, and I felt great. From that point I understood that it was lifestyle rather than a period of my life, so I have never stopped since then.I got into self-improvement coaching thanks to the Charlie Morgan's course (still watching it).I couldn't call myself a coach yet, since I haven't had any client yet, but I started following che course about a month ago.Right now I'm in a stage where I'm focusing on fixing my relationships and skills, and looking back I see that I have made a huge progress.

author: Rob Nölken
text: Hi fellow human!My name is Rob from the Netherlands, almost 53 winters young.In 2018 I had for the 3th time an inflammation and Perferation of my intestines. They had to take 35 cm out. My beloved lifestyle of sex, drugs and Rock n Roll almost took a toll on me.A year later I struggled with a burnout. I had to Get Sh!t Done! Wich is the name of my self developed course and community.I developed the course first of all for me, to keep me accountable and to work on the best version of myself. But I started to help other people aswell. In 2020 I started Disorganized Productions and Workinprogress as a company.I have everything set up, website, affiliate marketing, and do Podcasts (Chaos creates Harmony). I have a certificate as a "expert of experience" and do a study now to become a Holistic Health expert.Helping others to become the best version of themselves is my life purpose.I live, since almost a year in my Volvo(Ragnar) as a car-nomad, wich is the best time I ever!Mirroring, facing, embracing and loving my demons was the most important thing I did. And the most painful aswel. Facing and working on my trauma's, never ending story, haha.I think that Skool is the best platform to help me, so I can help others, to become the best version of themselves.Change life, before it changes you!Here is a little video about me:https://youtu.be/564W2ncmN8A?=siA8VPq2pFJlb7PrdcLove to connect with you, fellow human!Warm geertings,Rob3:42

author: Arthur Maddocks-Brown
text: Hey Guys!You may have seen my other post... so I PROMISE to keep this one shorter!After 15 years of depression and suicidal thoughts, the loss of my wife, house, dog, job, life, friends (everything) I hit rock bottom.I had a choice.Die or LiveIn that momentI chose to live..Not just EXISTBut Fucking LIVE!!!I studied life coaching and got 2 x certifications (Jay Shetty and Expert Coaching certifications)I got my breathwork, trauma and nervous system informed certificates as well as cold water immersion certification.When I started learning about coaching, in order to get my qualifications, I needed to coach people. I took what I learnt and started coaching (internationally)I fell in love with helping people. It made me feel good and it helped me heal.I've been in the coaching space now for 3 years!I am still in the building phase, but it's growing and opportunities are popping up.As soon as I got out my way... these things started to flow better.. I certainly could do better though!

I'm Jamie from Virginia Beach in the U.S.I got into self-improvement when I signed up for fitness coaching back in 2023The pain that got me into it was that I didn't bounce back from the weight I gained while my wife was pregnant with our second kid and we both needed to lose some fat so that we could keep up with our kidsI started coaching the beginning of last year because I saw a need for people to know what I learned through it (and the cool connections that I found from my 10+ years of Bible study and how self-improvement is at the core of it)1 yearI've gone through a lot of iterations of niches, my current path (which I feel good about) is the fitness one. I tried mindset, habit formation, and identity focus all of which are suppose to be the HOW of what I deliver, not the WHAT I deliver (which took me a year to figure out 🤷‍♂️)

author: Miguel Ariza Lora
text: What's up gang,My name is Miguel, 25 years old and Spanish-Dutch currently living in Thailand.My journey in self-improvement began around 15 years old when I was overweight. I really wanted to be more like the others my age. Luckily this is when YouTube began to take off and people starting sharing a lot of value around fitness and nutrition. Trial and error got me to losing 20kg's eventually, and I've been able to keep most of it off ever since. When I saw putting some work in could give me results, I realized the same thing could be done in other aspects.The following years I became a lot more extroverted, social and confident. Through studying social work and psychology I got into coaching people to build a better life. I was good doing those jobs, and now I've taken it online and moved to Thailand. Felt like a good next step in my self-improvement. Have been coaching people now for 7 years already. Learned a whole lot. About people, training techniques, but probably even more about myself.I just think the self-improvement journey is amazing, and I wish everyone can experience seeing themselves improve into a better version and better life, step by step. Does it ever end? I don't think so. There's always room for improvement. It can feel tiring at times, but I wouldn't want to have it any other way.Can't wait to connect more with all of you guys.- Miguel

author: Thomas El Nadir
text: Yo, Thomas here, soon-to-be 33 years old, French-Moroccan man currently living in Morocco.Can you briefly share from A to Z how you got into self-improvement?It all started at 17 when i wanted a girlfriend and googled "how to be more attractive to women" or something like that. I stumbled upon the "Game" community, the high achievers of the dating world, and oh boy it took me in quite the journey. This ultimately led me to understand how to attract and be charismatic not only from the outside but the inside, spirituality, and many epic adventures that got FAR beyond the dating world I initialy began with.What was the pain that got you into self-improvement?Loneliness and desire to connect (with women, at first).How did you get into self-improvement coaching/consulting?I went to more than a decade of life-changing experiences. Picked up many gems along the way that I realized with time would help so many people in the same struggle I was at 17 (and didn't go through what I went through to solve it).How long have you've been a self-improvement coach?It's been 7 years on-and-offAt what stage of your journey are you? (no shame if you're just getting started, we all got to start somewhere).Getting back to the coaching business with even more depth and background. And finally get rid of the shiny object syndrome that was such a curse of mine for that many years. Now that it's gone, i'm determined to build a powerful life for myself, and I have one more overcome to help people get out of to my arsenal ;)

author: Scott Northwolf
text: If your offer is a one liner like "I take self-improvement coaches from $0 to $10K per month in 42 days with my 'Reverse Engineered $10K Method' or they don't pay" then you do tell it even to your neighboor because you lose nothing and create massive curiousity by putting it out there every time you open your mouth.Creating content is the slowest and most inefficient way to get calls but it's the best for the long-term, be prepared to do it consistently and daily if possible for 7 months to 3 years.Cold and warm outreach is the fastest and most powerful way because you get to target precisely your niche, but that, of course, assumes you have locked in PERFECTLY your niche, you know exactly how they look, where they hang out, what's their language and their desired outcome. You need to be able to describe their day to day from the top of your head if I were to ask you at a moment's notice.Running ads with a proven startegy and making less thatn $50K per month is basically insanity, I have seen it a thousand times and some of the best players in the market keep saying it, if you don't have $10K every month that you can literally BURN and forget about them testing different startegies for ads you are throwing money into the trash bin.The ICP is still too broad and doesn't give emotional triggers to the potential audience but it's a good place to start, it's definetely better than nothing.This is the no bullshit answer and I know it sounds harsh but I hope you can take it objectively and analyze these points.I hope it helps, brother.Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brotner! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

But main aim has gotta be grinding that cold of course :) Content Creation right now is very basic just to lure in the icp. author: Lea Newkirk
title: Protecting the Focus.
text: I’ve been reading Deep Work by Cal Newport (recommended by @Scott Northwolf ), and it’s shifted how I look at the goals we’ve been reverse-engineering together. It made me realize that if we want to hit those big targets we talk about on our Friday calls, we can't afford to have our attention fractured. We’re all putting in so much work to stay in a high-frequency state, but I’ve noticed how easily "busy-ness" and multitasking can pull us right back down into the noise. To manifest the version of life we’re aiming for, I’m learning that we have to be just as intentional with our focus as we are with our vision.  I’m curious, what’s your take on this? Do you feel like multitasking is a necessary skill for where we're going, or is it just a distraction from the deep work we're actually here to do?

author: Lea Newkirk
title: ◀️Moving Backward
text: I’m still sitting with what @Scott Northwolf taught on the call about Reverse Engineering our goals, and honestly, it’s already changing my life. Instead of looking at where I am now and trying to climb the mountain toward a goal, I’ve been practicing sitting in the frequency of already having achieved it, and then looking back at the steps I took to get there. It shifts everything from "I hope I can do this" to "it is already a done." It makes the path feel so much more inevitable and way less overwhelming.  I’m curious, what’s one goal you’re currently reverse-engineering? If you look back from the "finished" version of your goal, what was the very first step you took to get there?

author: Scott Northwolf
title: Don't try to escape 😵‍💫
text: If you are trying to escape the present moment you are failing in real time.  The masculine error lies on believing that things will be fundamentally different some day.  "Someday my women will stop complaining."  "Someday I'll make enough money and I'll finally be free."  "Someday I'll land a client and everything will change forever."  Guess what?  When you land a client...  You still got to keep doing outreach.  You still got to keep building upwards.  I know because it happened to me.  Don't make the mistake of trying to escape your current situation.  If you stay on this path for long enough you WILL become rich and you WILL miss the days in which you were struggling and striving to hit these goals.  Enjoy the process now.  I know it sounds like a cliche but if you do that and let go of all atatchment to the outcome the inputs will become effortless and everything will flow without frustration.  Delusion backed up just by wo

text: I’m curious to know… how does your day looks like? text: Depends a bit, but usually I wake up with the birds, sometimes meditate but for sure gratitude. Before I go to work I be on Skool and do my emails. Then go to work. author: Scott Northwolf
text: @Rob Nölken I love the flow… sounds quite natural and effortless.There are two main modes of approaching your routine (neither is better, they depend on what’s your goal and what are you optimizing for):1-Intentional routine (a routine you design yourself with your goals in mind).Pros: you can reverse engineer your goal into actionable steps that you can take every day and it’s impossible to not hit it with enough time and understanding how to reiterate your process and take in feedback).Cons: It can suffocate inspiration if not properly managed or balanced with seasons of rest.2-Unintentional routine (it’s basically the routine that forms when we are adapting [effectively and productively or ineffectively and counterproductively] to our environment and internal state).Pros: Great for creativity and inspiration.Cons: It can turn into instant gratification, chaos and too much of a lack of structure that doesn’t allow for consistency or the creation and maintenance of anything (so goals are quite hard to hit in this way.And of course you can even have a combination of the two…

text: Right now - waking 03-05 and i just get into the work, building the business in a continuous flow for 8-12 hours - with brain breaks including anything from 2-35minute sessions of my own methodics blending yogic practices, breathwork, other deep nervous system techniques, nature walks, climbing a tree/doing some calisthetic movement - qi gong - yoga nidra - minor content creation (partially filming parts of these micro-longer session breaks including subjects, documenting it for later content). Right now grinding the computer hard to set up systems to soon be doing much more client work 1:1 than currently - which also includes the same kind of practices with clients between the talking/consulting them, depending on their need in the moment. With client sessions i also get a fair amount of my own practices in while instructing them digitally. Rapping/singing/dancing(a lot of my own tension relief and recovery comes from those three). All this really happens in a flow throughout the day in between computer work as is needed to stay balanced and focused. Sleep time about 21-23.

author: Andrew Brooks
title: Food quality in restaurants: probably not for you
text: How often do you go out, eat somewhere, and think to yourself "well, that wasn't great." And how did you feel afterwards?  There are reasons for the generally low quality of food in most restaurants. Mostly they are limited by their food budget. And the food budget limits the quality they are able to purchase because we also generally want cheap food. What that means is that most of the produce will be conventional produce, not organic. The meats (a large part of the budget) will be of lower quality and the rest will be medium quality at best.  The things we WANT: organic, fresh, healthy, and delicious, simply are not available within the cheap food framework. Unfortunately to get the good stuff you have to pay more. That's just the way it is for now...31Last comment Feb 24

title: Introducing Myself!!

Aged 15 diagnosed with depression, lived the next 15 years with depression and suicide ideation, multiple times a day I would have consistent and persistent thoughts.  2. In 2019 I got engaged to my partner of 7 years (aged 27)  3. We got a dog (2019) 4. COVID happened (2020)  5. We bought some land (2020) 6. October 2020 I was hit by a car   7. We built a house, got married, moved cities and changed jobs (2021)  8. 2021 cracks started forming in the relationship  9. 2022 Christmas I broke down about my mental heath challenges, my physical rehab journey and...  10. 2023 (January) she told me she was leaving...  11. author: Scott Northwolf
title: Not a community but...🛡️ Brotherhood!!!
text: I don't know about you but my aims are not to meet people randomly and never see them again even when we actually connected very deeply.  Consistency is not only applicable to your work, your self-improvement and to making progress on those areas of your life.  I believe consistency must be applied to one of the areas that matters the most in our lives, especially as men which is building a strong network of men whom we can ACTUALLY call brothers, and even though I do not believe only with calls and cool conversations something like that can be achieved... it's a start to build trust and get to know each other, see if our values align and if we can actually work together to build something greater than ourselves because THAT'S where true brotherhood is created:  Men with shared values struggling and striving together to achieve a common goal.  So I'll be DMing you guys perdiodically to jump on one on

author: Scott Northwolf
text: Welcome to the gang, Bruno. Glad to have you with us, brother!One of the biggest things for me was when I stopped thinking about what I wanted and started thinking in terms of what other people wanted and needed.Choosing a niche, going to them and actually asking them consistently and in a methodical way what were their dreams and hopes and what were their biggest problems and using that to build a truly attractive offer that they'd resonated with and they actually wanted to buy and then coming back and offering that to them.And if you picked right the niche and you were able to find them to conduct niche research interviews with them... you'll be able to find them again on the same place and get them on sales' calls with you.Then all that remains is to conduct appointment setting, sales' calls, build a good script and funnel structure and track your KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) in order to improve over time, stay consistent and don't switch niches or business models all together because that resets your progress back to 0.

author: Sajjad Bablu
title: From Content → Conversations → Real Change (What I’m Testing Right Now)
text: Over the last weeks I’ve been studying something interesting:  Most people online are creating content for visibility Very few are creating systems for transformation  Likes don’t change a person. Motivation videos don’t change behavior. Even good advice rarely sticks.  So I started experimenting with a different approach.  Instead of posting random self-improvement content, I’m building a structured flow:  1. A problem people recognize immediately 2. A guided reflection 3. A daily actionable step 4. Consistency over motivation  The goal isn’t engagement. The goal is clarity + behavior change.  Right now I’m testing how people respond when the focus shifts from inspiration → guidance  Not selling anything here — just observing behavior patterns and learning what actually helps people follow through in real life.  Curious: Do you think people actually want transformat

author: Scott Northwolf
text: This is very interesting, brother. Hope it goes well and please do share what you find out with this approach.Personally I have discovered that people do not value what they get for free for the most part.True transformation I have only seen it taking place through high ticket programs because then they are putting skin on the game, they are committed to make a change and there's real world consecuences to them not doing so (especially if you structure your offer with a powerful full money back conditional guarantee or even a risk reversal one which are usually the best but only when you get to a certain level on the game).This is a clear breakdown of it with some estimate projections that reflect this point:📊 Success (Completion) Rates by Program TypeProgram TypeTypical Completion Rate (Success Proxy)Free programs~5–15 % complete Low-ticket programs (e.g., paid but no heavy support)~15–40 % complete High-ticket programs (e.g., with coaching/community support)~60–80 %+ completeI actually took the time to dig into this data because I have been seeing this patter for a while and I was wondering if there were any studies about it and there are!It's insane but it's true!Real transformation happens when people are in enough pain so their current situation and staying in it is actually more painful than the effort and sacrifice of taking action consistently and changing radically their lives.I hope understanding this helps you to focus your efforts on the right direction, brother, because I did struggle with this for quite a while, running a lot of free case studies that would end up with VERY high churn and barely any results out of a lack of work and consistency from my mentees but reality is how it is, and I could have sat down and complained about it but instead I decided to accept reality as it is and actually look for the most effective approach to make a meaningful and profound impact on people's lives.If you want to make such impact you either need insane amounts of traffic so this low completion rates get to represent a lot more people than on low traffic enviroments, or you have to switch to a model in which the low traffic is actually very successful because the system is built in such a way that is conductive to VERY high standards and results driven by the level of committment of people (which is what I do and what I personally focus on).Delusion backed up by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brother. Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!

author: Scott Northwolf
text: @Aeon Bancuyo alright! So, let's break it down:You are afraid of charging high ticket.Why is that?Is it because you feel like your services are no valuable enough?Then tell me...How much do you feel comfortable with charging (no number is wrong, even if it's 0 at the moment)?Now...whatever that number is...Think...how can you make that number 10 times higher?What kind of MASSIVE value could you provide in order to make it worth that amount of money for people?That's a first step towards reframing this limiting belief.It's a basic framework but it will give you an idea of how much you can improve in order to be able to charge the kind of prices that would not just change your life but your clients' lives as well, because the higher ticket they pay the higher the percieved value they'll see in your offer, the higher their commitment and the higher their likelihood of achieving their desired outcome.The second question is a two part thing.First comes clarity in what you offer and then comes the confidence to state the value behind it.Without that clarity is very hard to express confidently how much are you charging for it, because it's hard to see the value when things are chaotic and confusing and confidence is nothing but a proxy for competence.So we start by actually having a VERY well defined outcome (that aligns with the percieved pain/desired of your prospects) enticing enough for the people in your niche (of course, there are certain foundations to this like picking properly the niche, conducting niche research interviews to actually understand in depth the percieved pain your potential clients have and their desired outcome, but I'll be talking in more depth about the roadmap to $10K per month that we follow here on self-improvement Nation soon to make all of this more clear, I've been asked already a lot for this and it's going to be very useful to our members who haven't spoke to me frequently).With regards to the third one.The fact that you are afraid of paying for mentorship is not really about mentors out there (even though obviusly like in all market there are good ones and shittier ones) but it speaks about how you view yourself and ties back to the fear of having people paying you for your services because you don't believe yet your services are that valuable or perhaps that you can deliever effectively, and if you don't believe (yet) in your own coaching... how can you believe in someone else's?This is just the beginning but I hope it does offers you some clarity and makes you reflect in depth about this subject.I'd recommend deep journaling about it and if you have any questions feel free to reach out, Aeon.Like I said, there's a lot more but take it step by step. It's all about consistency.Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called faith, brother! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

author: Scott Northwolf
title: Are you restless❓
text: Radical change doesn't happen in a vacuum.  Radical change only happens when the pain of staying the same outweights the pain of change.  There's a certain kind of restlessness that is required for such change to take place. Sleepless nights, doubts, fears... frustration? Meeting with reality and having to accept the world is simply NOT how you thought it was. So what will you do now? Take it as insight and modify your behavoir to actually get what you want or stay down, in regret, failure...  People think Self-Improvement is a walk of roses, all about positive mentality and some soft meditation but this is a FUCKING WAR TO THE DEATH with your old self.  There's no pleasure without pain and too much pleasure in the end will bring more pain than you could ever had imagine.  What do you really want out of this life?  Have you ever stopped to ask that to yourself?  And if so...  Have you ever wondered why?  I'll be reading VE

author: Paul Hedron
title: Basic Leadership Primer
text: The basic components of a team are as follows:  A group of individuals, a vision, and a facilitator to direct effort and attention.  A leader must be an expert in the nature of the vision, the individuals on the team, and the conditions in which the vision and team interact.  Without a vision or goal, the collection of individuals cannot coalesce. Without individuals to coalesce, a vision or goal is without potential and impetus. Without understanding of the conditions, a team will experience unnecessary inertia, spending effort to achieve the vision.  A vision is merely a blueprint, something to build. The individuals on the team are the builders. The leader is the architect and facilitator.  The base component of a team and shared vision is the individual.  By understanding the individual, a leader has better command of complexity.  An individual has a capable skillset, and limitations. Understanding both the capability/capacity,

author: Scott Northwolf
text: When you talk about turning individuals into leaders, do you mean creating something like individual autonomous cells, like a decentralized organization?The thing is, I believe this can be either interpreted as giving enough understanding and power to each individual so they develop themselves and become truly useful members for the tribe OR as pseudo-anarchic kind of organization, a decentralized thing, kind of like the internet phenomenon of Anonymous.With the first interpretation, building upwards members of the tribe and getting them to a place of taking absolute personal responsibility for their lives, being capable of autonomously and sustainably grow and have their own systems in place for their development and for yielding consistently results for themselves and for moving forwards the cause of the tribe… that’s powerful and aligns with my vision and naturally will always align with the vision of any self-improvement mentor.But decentralized structures and multiple cells of command only work for very specific kinds of purposes and environments.I don’t believe we have the need to over-think all this at the moment.Things like culture, organization and logistics and politics tend to be better put into practice by empirical testing and exploration, especially at the beginning stages.

author: Aeon Bancuyo
title: What do you do to keep being social?
text: I’m curious do you have friends or do you interact often with people not on self improvement?  I thought about the picture of the about page that @Scott Northwolf has, and I would love to have that kind of friend group as well.  Honestly, I’ve been in pain my whole life for not having a “stable” friend group, is this normal?  My friends circle we’re only 3 at a time, and it would die off overtime.  Currently, having not deep relationships with my school friends.  There’s so many people who I can be friends with in school, it’s too easy.  But I have a limiting belief that, theyre not worth it. Honestly, I get the “ick” when I’m trying to fit in to the gossip conversations, or the shallow “issue” of the day.  What do you do to battle this, I have a two friends that I have a deep relationship with but not at school.  What do you do to keep being social, even with people outside of self imorovemnt?35Last comment Jan 29

author: Scott Northwolf
text: I'm not really social with people who are not into self-improvement to be honest. The beauty about Bali is that EVERYONE (or at least most people) are into self-improvement so that makes it perfect but I know this is a very rare place, that's also the reason I moved here.Back in the day, when I was still back home I had a big group of friends but when I got into self-improvement I just stopped having things in common with them, our interests just weren't the same and even though I tried to get them on self-improvement I failed. You cannot force people to improve their lives if they don't want to.So basically I endured the time in solitude and used it to not be afraid of being alone and carve my own path, I also used it as motivation to make a shit ton of money and start traveling the world in order to find my tribe which I successfully did here in Bali.On self-improvement you'll go through this phase of loneliness in which you are not high status enough yet to be friends with the people you want to be friends in real life because they are still way above you but if you use that as motivation to get to their level you will get there, it's just a matter of time and commitment.I know it's hard but it's worth it and there's no going back once you take the red pill anyways.The only option is to keep moving forward, brother. Use this community to remind yourself of the people you'll have around you when you make it to the other side and don't lose faith.A lot of us are living here already and we have fulfilled the dream we were shooting for... now we are building together towards a higher aim and I can tell you there's nothing better than making your dreams come true with effort, sacrifice and dedication.

author: Aeon Bancuyo
text: @Scott Northwolf thanks for this man!I'll keep this as a reminder, but do you think there's a way for me to be not a social nerd and just make myself isolated, whilst on self improvement?

author: Scott Northwolf
text: @Aeon Bancuyo the funny thing about social skills is that you can actually develop them more by developing yourself and creating a rock solid confidence born out of REAL competence than by simply "doing reps" and talking to normies non-stop. It's the highest leverage thing you can do. By developing this sense of pride on yourself and having high standards for the people around you, counter-intuitively you become magnetic to people. I know the instant gratification pull of being social is strong but I can promise you that locking in and developing yourself as a man will only bring net benefits to your life and will not turn you into a "social weirdo" or a "self-improvement nerd" umless you hold such limiting belief and turn it into a self fulfilling profecy.

author: Andrew Brooks
text: I'm not really "social" either. I have about 3 good friends in my locale, and I have others that I connect with virtually every week. But I do feel the need to be social often.So, I invite friends over for dinner, plant to meet up for a coffee or a movie, or just meet up to hang out somewhere for an hour. It doesn't have to be a big thing.But I do plan fun things intentionally. I keep track of the artsy/cultural things happening in my area and will often just show up to some of those and see if I can chat with a new person there. And I will sometimes invite people I meet to connect with me at the event. Sometimes they show, sometimes not. But I'm ok doing things by myself too.So don't feel weird. You aren't. Especially as we get older, the number of friends typically dwindles, but the QUALITY of the friendships goes up. And that's ok.

author: J'son Elizondo
title: The Rise of AI Slop (And the Death of Real Synthesizers)
text:
𝗔𝗶 𝗦𝗹𝗼𝗽.. 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗗𝗲𝗮𝗱 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗲𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗦𝘆𝗻𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘀𝗶𝘇𝗲𝗿 𝗣𝗼𝘀𝘁𝘀..
Yeah I said it Synthesizer is being filled with articles written by BOTS! 🤖
Funniest thing I've seen is 2 articles back to back written by two separate people, same message almost the exact wording. (Not funny at all actually)
The information in this place used to be 𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗲, 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗿𝗮𝘄..
More value per syllable than any other community..
But now its being littered with em dashes, regurgitated nonsense and oh yea my favorite one, intellectual parroting (sarcasm).
The dead internet theory is a concept predicting that the internet will become predominantly composed of AI-generated content and bot activity, rather than human interaction..
The sad part is that WE are spreading this ai slop. (Literally doing the bid of AI).
By increasing the pace at which it swallows, LITERALLY engulfs the internet whole.
We are masquerading.
Devaluing our own authenticity.
Impersonating what it's like to be a human.
I want to share that community is built on connection and connection is real and raw.
And RAW makes mistakes. Those mistakes in the moment to you, might seem like flaws or blemishes that should be covered. But I want to argue that it is those blemishes are what will distinguish YOU from all of the other articles, posts and NOW 𝗔𝗜- 𝗚𝗘𝗡𝗘𝗥𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗 𝗦𝗟𝗢𝗣 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗚𝗥𝗔𝗠𝗦.. <-- (Can you believe that ?!)
⭐Studies now show that we even speak like ai ⭐
Tell me if you've heard this.
"Not only is this not x, But its Y"
"Its not x but Y"
And words like DELVE 🤣😆😂
The Rule of Threes. 𝗘𝘅. ( Clear, concise, and compelling) (I DONT even feel right using the rule of threes in formulating my writing because I think people will perceive it as AI. (Even though I speak like that naturally as a philosophy reader)
Its naturally rolling off of our tongues at this point because we consume it SO OFTEN.
ONLY increasing the very PROBLEM that this community aims to solve in the first place..
🌊"𝗪𝗲 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗱𝗿𝗼𝘄𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗻 𝗶𝗻𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗯𝘂𝘁 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝘄𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺"🌊
With every article generated by ai you are effectively adding MORE water to the already filled bucket.
I want to share with you that not polishing an article, post or comments is actually going to 𝗵𝗲𝗹𝗽 𝘆𝗼𝘂, 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝘆𝗼𝘂.
🤮Articles on social media migration and social media rebellion states: "THEY ARE LEAVING BECAUSE IT NO LONGER 𝗙𝗘𝗘𝗟𝗦 𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟" SAYING IT 𝗙𝗘𝗘𝗟𝗦 𝗔𝗥𝗧𝗜𝗙𝗜𝗖𝗜𝗔𝗟, 𝗨𝗡𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟 𝗔𝗡𝗗 𝗠𝗔𝗡𝗜𝗣𝗨𝗟𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗.. "IT FEELS LIKE A 𝗙𝗔𝗞𝗘 𝗘𝗡𝗩𝗜𝗢𝗥𝗡𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧 𝗖𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗 𝗕𝗬 𝗔 𝗠𝗔𝗖𝗛𝗜𝗡𝗘" ..🤮
So with all that in mind..
Think before you use ai.
Think before you let it write your articles for you.
And for god sakes don't let it write your whole program from conception to birth, so you can COLD pitch it to someone FOR money.
𝗧𝗛𝗔𝗧 𝗶𝘀 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗯𝗲𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗳𝘂𝗹 𝗺𝗼𝗻𝗲𝘆. 𝗧𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝘀𝗰𝗮𝗺.
A NOT so clever scam that aims to take advantage of people who are in pain and need experts, 𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟 𝗦𝗬𝗡𝗧𝗛𝗘𝗦𝗜𝗭𝗘𝗥𝗦, 𝗡𝗢𝗧 𝗔𝗜 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗺𝗽𝘁 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗳𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹𝘀.

author: Des Cooke
text:
I have a close contact who is a content writer, a ghost writer essentially. One of the things the ghostwriting community has been saying for a very long time is that AI will never replace them because of the fact that you can witness ai content, as you say you can see it.
One of the recent developments is that that person has now got an AI machine essentially that's taken time to build.
That machine is trained on lots of a person's content, like:
it's trained on their videos
it's trained on their podcasts
it's trained on their blog posts, etc.
They can literally talk into their phone about a story, an idea, a thought, a meeting. "I've just had a conversation with the director about blah blah blah blah blah, and by the time they get back to the desk, there's a post already written that takes almost no editing!
It is now spitting out posts that are relevant to the arena the person works in, providing it's trained enough and that the input posts are actually good enough.
Someone like me has decades of back catalogue.
That ghost writer is moving f̲r̲o̲m̲ ghost writing,
with the community of people in ghost writing saying "AI will never replace them",
t̲o̲ delivering people access to this server that can be trained on their back catalogue so that it can replace their posts within the content that they actually have.
I suppose this is a bit like using Notebook LM, etc., but this seems to be much more advanced, and I've seen some of the output.
Initially, he was completely distraught and downhearted that he'd built himself out of a job, but this would actually be a product with the hundreds of files put together and the person accessing it remotely linking their own content for it to be trained on. Essentially, a cost to set it up, and be trained how to use it and train it - then the customer could run it on their own machine.
It's actually as good as he can do now as a ghostwriter and is indistinguishable from his own written content, even by someone like him who notices the normal AI slop a lot.
As Ferris Bueller said: "𝘓𝘪𝘧𝘦 𝘮𝘰𝘷𝘦𝘴 𝘱𝘳𝘦𝘵𝘵𝘺 𝘧𝘢𝘴𝘵. 𝘐𝘧 𝘺𝘰𝘶 𝘥𝘰𝘯'𝘵 𝘴𝘵𝘰𝘱 𝘢𝘯𝘥 𝘭𝘰𝘰𝘬 𝘢𝘳𝘰𝘶𝘯𝘥 𝘰𝘯𝘤𝘦 𝘪𝘯 𝘢 𝘸𝘩𝘪𝘭𝘦, 𝘺𝘰𝘶 𝘤𝘰𝘶𝘭𝘥 𝘮𝘪𝘴𝘴 𝘪𝘵."
It'll be a matter of weeks, possibly months, before the world has caught up with him. And you won't be able to tell?
Question: Did I write this with AI?

author: J'son Elizondo
title: The Rise of AI Slop (And the Death of Real Synthesizers)
text:
𝗔𝗶 𝗦𝗹𝗼𝗽.. 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗗𝗲𝗮𝗱 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗲𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗦𝘆𝗻𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘀𝗶𝘇𝗲𝗿 𝗣𝗼𝘀𝘁𝘀..
Yeah I said it Synthesizer is being filled with articles written by BOTS! 🤖
Funniest thing I've seen is 2 articles back to back written by two separate people, same message almost the exact wording. (Not funny at all actually)
The information in this place used to be 𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗲, 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗿𝗮𝘄..
More value per syllable than any other community..
But now its being littered with em dashes, regurgitated nonsense and oh yea my favorite one, intellectual parroting (sarcasm).
The dead internet theory is a concept predicting that the internet will become predominantly composed of AI-generated content and bot activity, rather than human interaction..
The sad part is that WE are spreading this ai slop. (Literally doing the bid of AI).
By increasing the pace at which it swallows, LITERALLY engulfs the internet whole.
We are masquerading.
Devaluing our own authenticity.
Impersonating what it's like to be a human.
I want to share that community is built on connection and connection is real and raw.
And RAW makes mistakes. Those mistakes in the moment to you, might seem like flaws or blemishes that should be covered. But I want to argue that it is those blemishes are what will distinguish YOU from all of the other articles, posts and NOW 𝗔𝗜- 𝗚𝗘𝗡𝗘𝗥𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗 𝗦𝗟𝗢𝗣 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗚𝗥𝗔𝗠𝗦.. <-- (Can you believe that ?!)
⭐Studies now show that we even speak like ai ⭐
Tell me if you've heard this.
"Not only is this not x, But its Y"
"Its not x but Y"
And words like DELVE 🤣😆😂
The Rule of Threes. 𝗘𝘅. ( Clear, concise, and compelling) (I DONT even feel right using the rule of threes in formulating my writing because I think people will perceive it as AI. (Even though I speak like that naturally as a philosophy reader)
Its naturally rolling off of our tongues at this point because we consume it SO OFTEN.
ONLY increasing the very PROBLEM that this community aims to solve in the first place..
🌊"𝗪𝗲 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗱𝗿𝗼𝘄𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗻 𝗶𝗻𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗯𝘂𝘁 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝘄𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺"🌊
With every article generated by ai you are effectively adding MORE water to the already filled bucket.
I want to share with you that not polishing an article, post or comments is actually going to 𝗵𝗲𝗹𝗽 𝘆𝗼𝘂, 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝘆𝗼𝘂.
🤮Articles on social media migration and social media rebellion states: "THEY ARE LEAVING BECAUSE IT NO LONGER 𝗙𝗘𝗘𝗟𝗦 𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟" SAYING IT 𝗙𝗘𝗘𝗟𝗦 𝗔𝗥𝗧𝗜𝗙𝗜𝗖𝗜𝗔𝗟, 𝗨𝗡𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟 𝗔𝗡𝗗 𝗠𝗔𝗡𝗜𝗣𝗨𝗟𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗.. "IT FEELS LIKE A 𝗙𝗔𝗞𝗘 𝗘𝗡𝗩𝗜𝗢𝗥𝗡𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧 𝗖𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗 𝗕𝗬 𝗔 𝗠𝗔𝗖𝗛𝗜𝗡𝗘" ..🤮
So with all that in mind..
Think before you use ai.
Think before you let it write your articles for you.
And for god sakes don't let it write your whole program from conception to birth, so you can COLD pitch it to someone FOR money.
𝗧𝗛𝗔𝗧 𝗶𝘀 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗯𝗲𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗳𝘂𝗹 𝗺𝗼𝗻𝗲𝘆. 𝗧𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝘀𝗰𝗮𝗺.
A NOT so clever scam that aims to take advantage of people who are in pain and need experts, 𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟 𝗦𝗬𝗡𝗧𝗛𝗘𝗦𝗜𝗭𝗘𝗥𝗦, 𝗡𝗢𝗧 𝗔𝗜 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗺𝗽𝘁 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗳𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹𝘀.
Authenticity over curation
Authenticity over curation

author: Ailandas Sulcas
text:
I totally agree.
We start using tools that make life easier without realising that easier doesn't necessarily mean better. AI is a good example of that.
We use it to lighten the load on our brain but then we lose our ability to be analytical, logical, and intellectual.
It's good as a tool but it's bad as anything else.

author: Scott Northwolf
text:
Bro, I agree completely. I am a writter at heart and you and I have this beautiful obsession with knowledge and understanding, and I know you draw as much satisfaction from teaching and sharing with others your knowledge as I do... and it is quite literally like a drug for us... but in a good sense, because we get to connect very deeply with amazing people and we get to touch their lives in very impactful ways changing it for the better.
I'm really glad you are bringing this to people's attention because this is something I ALWAYS say to people: If you let AI write for you... I mean... can you really take pride on what "you wrote"? It's not even your own knowledge... and it's sad... because by teaching we solidify our knowledge.... by teaching we learn at the same time.
This is what we are here for and letting something as beautiful as that die would be a true shame.
At the same time... I don't think the AI slop is so bad. It's actually WONDERFUL! Because the more of it there is out there the more we will stand out for being authentically ourseleves and the more rare we'll be. We'll be the only ones willing to do the "hard work" of writting and teaching ourselves and we will benefit greatly personally from that.
The work works more on you than you work on it, and that's a fact, so when people chose the easy path, the path of least resistance they are missing out on the amazing gift that focus and deep work bring to their lives, the development of their discipline, the development of their capacity to actually sit in presence on the present moment and give their best to something beautiful and meaningful like it is connecting with people and helping them change their lives.
I truly loved this post. Thank you so much for sharing it with me, brother... and above all: thank you for being you, Jason, you are a shinning lighthouse in the middle of this dark sea and you have no idea of how deeply appreciated you are, man.
Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion, but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brother! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

author: Lea Newkirk
title: The "Learning" Loop 🔄
text:
For a long time, I was addicted to the feeling of potential.
I’d spend hours consuming manifestation videos and different types of self-improvement techniques, convinced I was making progress because I understood the concepts. But my life wasn't actually moving. My mind was racing ahead while my physical reality was standing still. I realized I was using "learning" as a safety blanket. It was a way to feel productive without ever having to risk the discomfort of actually putting myself out there. I was feeding my brain, but starving my results. I found that I didn’t actually need more videos or books. I needed to take the information out of your head and move it into my daily life.
I’m curious about your experience:
Have you ever used "researching" as a way to avoid the actual work?
What’s one action you’re taking today that doesn't involve a screen?
Would love to see where this discussion goes ✨

author: Scott Northwolf
text:
It's not a loop... it's a spiral...
It keeps progressing over time.
We flood our unconscious mind with knowledge until the unconscious surface to the conscious.
This is a good insight but there is no right or wrong approach, there's just different approaches depending on your ultimate goal.
Do you want to get results quicker?
Apply the scientific method.
Acquire enough knowledge so you can formulate a theory.
Grab that theory and go out there and test it.
Track the data you get out of it.
Analayze the feedback and readjust accordingly for the next reiteration to improve.
There are 3 components to the META-skill, they are:
1-Deliberate practice.
2-Reflection upon your practice.
3-Theoretical knowledge applied to your practice.
Applying correctly and consistently these 3 components you'll be able to gain mastery over ANY skill that you desire over time.
All you need to be sucessful is: Clarity (at least enough so you can take action intentionally), Commitment (a firm decision to follow through until you hit your goal) and consistency.
1-Set a goal and craft a theory with a minimum amount of critical knowledge.
2-Commit to such goal so you know you'll hit it sooner or later and decide not to stop until you do so.
3-Take action relentlessly to hit such goal and pay attention to the feedback by simply tracking what you are doing, analyzing your intentionality and understanding if it's beeing fulfilled or not and why so you can reiterate and improve your theory with such data.

author: Kasparas Stancikas
title: 🥊 First Ever Kickboxing Fight... (Knockout, Faked Documents, Work Ethic)
text:
Just before i tell you about the craziest 30 days of my life, i want to thank every single one of you for keeping me accountable under my daily accountability posts:)))
Thanks Synthesizers 🤝
THE STORY:
34 days ago my great friend told me:
"Kasparas, in a month there’s this amateur kickboxing competition."
2 seconds later, I said NO because right now i am:
Following synthesizer roadmap and thats my nr1 priority in life
i want to put all my focus into my business
Its too early I’ve only been training for 1 year
Excuses...
But as I continued with my day, this thought stuck in my head. At the end of the day i always had a dream to compete in atleast 1 martial arts fight
My gut intuition was getting stronger and stronger...
So I DMed my trainer to ask if I was even qualified to compete.
I got a response: “Only if you promise to train really damn hard.”
And that’s where it all started...
I quit bodybuilding and went all in on martial arts training 1-2 times a day and keeping myself accountable in synthesizer every single day for 30 days straight sharing how i am prepping for my fight + scaling my business all at the same time
There was one issue tho...
I still had no clue how would I get my parents to sign the agreement letting me fight? (i am 17yo)
My mom is really against fighting, she HATES it. My dad kind of does too. So, two weeks into training, when it was time to sign the papers, I did what any normal guy would do...
I faked the documents:D I forged my dad’s signature and everything.
My trainer had no clue, and it all worked out.
So 30 days later, after an insane amount of training and dieting (and still horrible cardio), I was about to step into the ring with another 81kg guy who was a year older and had 3 years of training to rip my head off
Round 1: Went really damn well. I was physically stronger, so I scared him away with some jabs and leg kicks.
Round 2: Still going decent, but I started getting extremely gassed out at the end.
Round 3: I had zero cardio left, luckily my opponent was hella gassed out too. This round was just to see who could survive the longest. And guess what? With only 10 seconds left, I landed a clear hook and KNOCKED HIM OUT, winning the god damn fight!
I still remember my friends screaming in the corner. (Watch the vid below.)
That was one of the BEST moments of my life...
I grew so much as a person, gained so much wisdom, and even managet to CLOSE MULTIPLE HIGH TICKET deals during the prep.

author: Scott Northwolf
text:
I agree completely.
First thing to understand is where people are amongst the 3 awareness levels.
Are they?
1-No awareness (they are in pain but don't know what's their problem).
2-Problem aware (they know they are in pain and know at least on a high level view what's their problem and starting to activeley look for solutions).
3-Solution awareness (know they have a problem and know what's the solution, now they are ready to take action on it).
Level 2 and 3 are the best parts.
That's the warmest leads you'll get.
Funnel structure:
1-At level one you call out their pain and attract them to your community (or YT channel or whatever).
2-At level 2 you get them inside your community and give them declarative knowledge so they start seeing what's their "enemy", their problem.
3-At level 3 you present them with the solution and they are ready to start taking actionable steps in order to solve it, usually this is best done on a sales' call already.
Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brother! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

author: Lukas Schmidt
title: I lost the Skool Games. Don't repeat my mistakes
text:
Since I joined Skool back in 2024, I wanted to win the Skool Games.
It is the closest thing to the business Olympics there is.
But that also means you compete against the best.
The best have a massive audience with teams and systems in place to scale a low-ticket offer.
(In the Skool Games, you can charge a maximum of $100 per month or $1200 per year)
I worked a lot more in 26 Q1 compared to 25 Q4, but made a lot less.
Still grateful for the experience and had a lot of fun.
But I wouldn't recommend it to anyone who is getting started.
In the beginning, it is much more effective to charge high ticket and scale as much as possible.
Instead of chasing the shiny trophy, I am committed to building a solid foundation.
I also found it to be much easier to help someone else win.
Congrats again to @Michael Rizk and @Jenna Kelly.
You guys crushed it.
I am also excited to be more active here again, since I am not locked into the competition.
Let's change the world together.
What are you focused on in Q2?

author: Scott Northwolf
text: I couldn't agree more, low-ticket is going to be harder to pull off, especially in the beginning because it requires massive amounts of traffic. With leverage and money you can build up that traffic and if you get that from high ticket that's the best way to go.

author: Piotr Rzepecki
title: Why Most People Never Make Real Money Online (And What Actually Changes Everything)
text:
Most people focus on the wrong things → content, followers, looking professional.
But none of that guarantees money.
Attention ≠ income. System = income.
Right now, most people operate like this:
Post → get views → maybe engagement → no sales
And then they think the problem is reach, algorithm, or niche.
It’s not.
The problem is there’s no structure behind what they’re doing. No path. No sequence. No flow.
Just random actions that don’t connect.
Here’s the shift you need to understand: Most money is NOT made on the first sale
Beginners think: I need one perfect product, I need one perfect offer
Reality is different.
First sale = entry point
Some people even go: Break even or lose money on the first offer → just to get the customer in
Why would anyone do that?
Because of this:
1 purchase → much higher chance of 2nd purchase
And that’s where the real money starts.
Not from convincing strangers once. But from serving people who already trust you.
So instead of thinking: How do I sell this one thing?
You need to think: How do I build a path someone can follow?
A simple structure looks like this:
Attention → content, posts, videos
Capture → something valuable in exchange for contact
Trust → consistency, follow-up, useful content
Offer → clear, relevant solution
More value → upsells, deeper or faster outcomes
That’s the system.
Another important reality:
~7 interactions → before someone buys
So if you post once and expect results → wrong expectationIf you post a few times and quit → you never even gave it a chance
People need to see you multiple times. They need to understand you. They need to trust you.
Trust is built through repetition.
That’s why follow-up matters so much.
Not aggressive selling. Not spamming links.
But showing up consistently, giving value, explaining things clearly, and slowly removing doubts.
Now here’s where most people lose again:
They finally get one sale → and stop there
That’s one of the biggest mistakes.
Because:
Best moment to sell again = right after someone buys
At that moment, trust is highest Resistance is lowest.
That’s why this structure works:
Main offer → upsell → downsell → additional value
Important rule:
Main product must already deliver value on its own.
Upsells are not there to fix it. They are there to enhance it.
Example:
Basic solution → advanced version Slow result → faster result Simple info → deeper system
All optional, all relevant.
Now what if someone says no?
Upsell X → Downsell
Lower price, Simpler version, Less friction
And many people who said no at first → say yes here
Because it feels like a better deal
This is not guesswork. This is based on how people actually buy.
Now zoom out again.
Everything starts with a simple entry point:
Value → attention → relationship
You give something useful. You attract the right people. You start building trust.
You’re no longer chasing random traffic.
You’re building a base:
Audience → trust → repeat buyers
That’s how long-term money is created.
Not from one post. Not from one product.
But from a system that keeps working.
Another mistake people make:
They build once → and never test
They assume everything works.
But small issues break everything:
Broken links, confusing offers, bad flow.
And every mistake = lost money
And you don’t get those people back
That’s why people who get results:
Test everything. Go through the process themselves. Fix problems early Improve step by step.
Because once traffic starts coming in, You need the system to work.
Now compare both approaches:
No system: Post → hope → no results
System: Attention → Trust → Offer → More value
One is random. One is controlled.
And control is everything.
Because when you control the system, You control the outcome.
You know what’s working. You know what’s not. You know what to fix.
And that’s when things start to scale.
So if you feel stuck:
It’s not your content. It’s not your niche. It’s not your potential.
It’s the lack of structure behind what you’re doing.
You don’t need more ideas.
You need a system that turns attention into results.
Build it once. Improve it over time.
And everything changes.

author: Scott Northwolf
text:
I'd expand on this:
"A simple structure looks like this:
Attention → content, posts, videos
Capture → something valuable in exchange for contact
Trust → consistency, follow-up, useful content
Offer → clear, relevant solution
More value → upsells, deeper or faster outcomes
That’s the system."
In the following way:
To actually draw attention consistently instead of posting into the void and hoping it will work or spending a lot of time honing your skills to guess what people like... I personally do this:
Select a niche (a group of people without stating their "problem", at this point I don't really know what their most painful percieved problem is).
Conduct niche research interviews (minimum from 14 to 30 but the more the better, but do not procrastinate by elongating this stage) using a script with questions to figure out what's their biggest percieved problem and their desired outcome (you'll pick up on the niche's language as well, this is important).
Build high ticekt offer with the knowledge that you now possess (you should have picked up on the most recurrent patterns) [you can use Alex Hormozi's framework from his book $100M Offers, that's a very good one for getting started].
Develop the methodology on the back-end of your offer in order to help people attain their desired outcome, if it feels like you don't even know if you'll be able to do it that's good, it means you'll have to grow and learn to be able to deliver consistently.
After selecting the niche properly and finding where they hang out and approaching them to get them on a niche research interview... guess what? You already have the skeleton of your outbound system. You just need to go back to the same place and ask them to jump on a call with you, if possible also gather them in one place like a skool community, an instagram page or an email list (this is where content for nurturing your audience becomes important, not saying that starting with content is a bad approach but this is just how I do it and it works for me).
Create sales' script (test for 30 reiterations minimum to know what are your conversion rates with a big enough data sample and when you make a change test always for 30 reiterations) [Jordan Belfort's The Way of Wolf is an amazing book for getting started creating your own sales' script].
Run structured sales' calls consistently and record them in order to optimize.
Track your KPIs (ABR, SUR, GFR and SCR).
Reverse engineer the numbers to your desired monthly income (I think I'll make a post about that soon).
Ok, so then to summerize:
Select niche.
Run niche research interviews (14 to 30).
Grand slam offer.
Methodology development.
Appointment setting (outbound system).
Sales' script development.
Record and optimize sales' calls.
Track your KPIs.
Reverse engineer your desired monthly income periodically using the KPIs data.

author: Mohammed Attar
title: My "Wall of Fame" (And the Systems Behind It)
text:
I see people celebrate wins with screenshots all the time. But screenshots get buried. They disappear in the feed.
I decided to make my wins permanent. These are three 5-star reviews from my best clients. I framed them and hung them on my wall.
Why? Because I need to remind myself of the standard of work I provide. Every day.
And when I’m on a call, or making a post like this, it’s not just words. It’s physical proof of authority.
The Strategy:
Identify your top 3 wins.
Turn them into tangible, physical objects.
Use them to dominate your environment and build authority.
Your wins deserve to be permanent.

title: Do you use Push or Pull Motivation?
text:
I saw this podcast by Alex Hormozi and Tony Robbins, and it's always stuck out to me the concept of Push versus Pull motivation.
(You can watch it here: https://youtu.be/xz-ymFOhBAE?si=9jY06iWkMi3vSZd1)
Push motivation is a reuiqrement, and obligation, a "have to" mindset that forces you to use willpower.
Pull motivation is a desire, a want, a purposeful NEED to do the thing, without require as much, or any willpower.
I saw this post by @Aathraey Shrikanth, asking what we would teach our younger selves, and I wouldn't "teach myself anything", but I would 100% want to see what would happen if I told myself to rely on pull instead of push.
(That post is here: https://www.skool.com/synthesizer/what-lesson-would-you-teach-your-younger-self)
What do you guys think about this, what are you running on, and what's your experience with the opposite kind of motivation (if any).

author: Scott Northwolf
title: What's the soul?
text:
@Michael Pluszek recently made a post that called my attention (https://www.skool.com/synthesizer/soul-vs-identity?p=21a05ba9) and I gave a brief introductory explanation of the structure of reality based on the Hermetic Tradition Model.
I called it part one of my answer and here it is:
For clarity and simplicities' sake I'll omit some details and break it down into a simplistic model developed by the ancient Hermetics.
Reality is structured in different layers of organization.
1-Source consciousness (the void, the one, what ancient Greeks in the Pythagorean tradition used to call the Monad).
2-The Mental Plain (The Duat).
3-The causal plain (where there's an observer, an object and a subject, you need three elements to have an experience, you need an object, a subject and an option, an experience itself).
4-The Astral Plain (what's called the Quantum Field of Possibilities, where ideas, language, feelings and imagination exist as components of reality we can percieve).
5-Physical reality (the manifestation and integration of all the above components).
To understand identity and the soul we need to understand first this structure because it's foundational to the understanding that flows from this. If we don't take this as the basis the rest will not make sense and to explain why this is so it will take... well, a lot of time, so let's just suspend disbelief and accept this framework as a precodition for the following knowledge to unfold itself.
If you want to get the full explanation just let me know and I'll write the second part of this...
///////////// ///////////// ///////////// ///////////// /////////////
Since some of you, guys, requested for the second part here it goes ( @Lea Newkirk @Victor Floden @Michael Pluszek ):
The soul is an unchanging configuration of pure consciousness, an emanation of the Source at the level of the mental and causal plain linking them both together.
Mental Plane → Soul → Causal Plane → Astral → Physical
The soul serves the function of incarnating into the physical plain, it has two main paths:
It will either descend into density by entangling itself with matter and the lower insticts generating karma or...
It will release karma through 'gnosis' (knowlege of its divine essence) and return to source through a purification process.
This is not as simple as I'm painting it to be.
Souls on their path out of source and back to it won't follow a linear downwards and then upwards trend during a cycle of reincarnations until EVERYTHING returns to source, it will look more like EVERY single possible combination ascending during some reincarnations, going 'downwards' during others and ascending again and like that on many different ways throughout all the possible reincarnations that can occur during a full cycle.
This cycles can be viewed as a cosmic breath of sorts.
I'd love to answer any questions that you guys might come up in the comments and clarify everything in case I haven't explained it with enough clarity for you.

author: Kai Cerar
text: Wow!! This is soooo deep man!!!

author: Scott Northwolf
text: @Kai Cerar yeah, I know... got some requests to talk about it... hope you got something useful out of it. I rather give actionable steps on how to use all of this for practical purposes but maybe this post will spark that kind of questions and I'd be glad to answer them.

author: Kai Cerar
text: @Scott Northwolf YES!! Actionable steps are the best man!

author: Scott Northwolf
text:
@Kai Cerar one of the most powerful things this can do for you is first of all shifting the mindset from the victim's perspective to the main character's perspective.
How would your life change if you knew that EVRYTHING that happens in your life is not happening TO you but FOR you and that you have chosen it all from your higher perspective in order for you to learn a lesson and grow through awareness.
Wouldn't it lift a huge weight from your shoulders to start 'playing' at life instead of struggling though it?
Detatchment from outcome creates a kind of confident and playful relaxation that allows us to face challenges with an unexorable and relentless mental fortitude...
This allows us to actually dream big and set huge goals but also reverse engineer them into actionable steps that we can undertake daily in order to acheive such dreams.
If you want you can share what's you ultimate goal for your life and we can map it into your current day to day to see how you are making progress consistently towards such goal.

author: Scott Northwolf
text:
@Kai Cerar maybe taking one full day to do a full-blown dopamine detox and journal very deeply about what you want out of this life will help.
I do an exercise with my clients for goal setting which is VERY powerful.
We humans are like ships, we set sail and go into the ocean, a lot of people end up stranded on a desert island wondering why they didn't become millionaires or something like that (let's say becoming a millionaire would be in this metaphor arriving at Bristol, England and let's say you set sail from Boston).
Well... did you ever set a goal to actually get there?
Imagine a ship going into the ocean without compass, without a map or a port of destination...
Hard to see meaning on the voyage if you don't really know where you are going.
Enjoying the process is a very important part of it but having a powerful motivation is a game changer.

author: Jack Shiller
text: Scott, I believe the causal plane (the plane of Will) is first. The correct order would be causal, mental, astral, physical

author: Scott Northwolf
text:
@Jack Shiller yeah, when I came across it for the first time I thought the same and I saw some traditions differing about it but i opted for the model of the Ancient Greeks (Pythagorians, Platonists, Neo-Platonists) and the Hermetics (as well as the Mediaval Alchemists, but those guys went crazy overcomplicating the whole thing)...
They explain that the structure is the following:
1-Monad (source, void).
2-Duat (Mental plain).
3-Triad (Causal plain: it would sound logical that if you have two things you can see cause an effect but in reality is not possible because if there's not a third 'thing', an observer then there's no choice to be made, there's no distinction between you and the other thing because there's no awareness of you as such if there's no separation, if there's no YOU, let's say, and also an awareness or consiousness component within you that is observing yourself and the other 'thing' with which you are interacting then there's no causality, I hope I explained myself correctly there).
4-Tetrad (Astral plain, the inifinte realm of possibilities, the quantum field of possibilities, ideas, concepts, emotions, imaginations, all that we can percieve through the third eye).
5-Pentad (Physical plain, 'material' reality, what Buddhists call Maya, illusion, the realm we percieve through our senses).
PS: That's the Tree of Life representing the Ten Sefirot from the Jewish Kabbalah. The Jewish Kabbalah is an esoteric tradition taken from the ancient Egyptian Prists and Farohs by Moses and revealed trough him to the Jewish people. It's good stuff. Most pathways lead to the top of the mountain. From the top the view is always the same.

author: Scott Northwolf
text: 3 things:1-Goal setting, drive vacuums identification and reverse engineering of your goal into actionable steps.2-After identifying your goal get inspiration and information about it (e.g. if you want to become a millionaire read the biographies of Andrew Carnegie and Rockefeller, if you want to become a military genious read Alexander's Campagins by Arian and Napoleon: A life).3-Identify your current beliefs with regards to the components of reality that you are struggling with and flip those beliefs in their head, build then a Self-Transcendence Document or Alchemy of Self Workbook.If you want me to explain in more depth those things just shoot me a DM. I know the third one is the "weirdest" one but it requires a LOT of explanation so it makes sense...The logical chain is like this:Experiences form beliefs.Beliefs form your identity.Your identity informs your paradigm (the lenses through which you see the world and interpret it).Your paradigm will inform your percieved options.Your percieved options will inform your actions (you can only make a decision out of the percieved options you believe are available to you).Your actions will solidify (only temporarily) into your behaviour (habits, routines [intentional or unintentional]).Your behaviour will determine your results.

author: Scott Northwolf
text: Alright! So let me break this down.For clarity and simplicities' sake I'll omit some details and break it down into a simplistic model developed by the ancient Hermetics.Reality is structured in different layers of organization.1-Source consciousness (the void, the one, what ancient Greeks in the Pythagorean tradition used to call the Monad).2-The Mental Plain (The Duat).3-The causal plain (where there's an observer, an object and a subject, you need three elements to have an experience, you need an object, a subject and an option, an experience itself).4-The Astral Plain (what's called the Quantum Field of Possibilities, where ideas, language, feelings and imagination exist as components of reality we can percieve).5-Physical reality (the manifestation and integration of all the above components).To understand identity and the soul we need to understand first this structure because it's foundational to the understanding that flows from this. If we don't take this as the basis the rest will not make sense and to explain why this is so it will take... well, a lot of time, so let's just suspend disbelief and accept this framework as a precodition for the following knowledge to unfold itself.If you want to get the full explanation just let me know and I'll write the second part of this...

author: Scott Northwolf
title: You vote with your behaviour, not your words...
text:
What are you saying to people you'll do but you are not actually doing and procrastinating on it?
How many times have you started with something just to drop it as soon as it gets hard?
When was the last time you stuck to something for MORE than an entire YEAR and actually did the WORK consistently?
Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relenstless action... that's called FAITH, brother! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!

title: What I learned after hitting 800 subscribers
text: Hey Synthesizers!  During the fall of 2024 I uploaded 7 videos for 12 weeks straight and ended up going from 200 -> 800 subscribers.  I did this while being a 27 year old married man, working a full time job.  After hitting 800 subscribers I noticed three things: - I was unsure about what to sell - I didn’t have a single client - I only booked one call  I was confused about what to do next.  I hopped on Skool and was reminded about a beautiful thing that exists.  “The Synthesizer Roadmap”  I put my trust in Andrew Kirby and hopped the following 5 months on 100 calls - 65 networking calls - 30 discovery calls - 5 sales calls  As a result I closed 2 online clients on Synthesized deals.  That’s because The Roadmap allowed me to get on calls and discover real life problems.  This is where I could leverage my proof in multiple areas and create customized solutions. author: Scott Northwolf
text: So this is basically what I have done:1-Picked a niche (people I really wanted to help and I identified with because I was them in the past, they were self-improvement coaches because that's what I was myself).2-Started conducting niche research interviews to figure out what was their problem and their desired outcome (didn't try to assume it, actually asked).3-With all the info gathered I build my offer and methodology ensuring good product to market fit.4- I developed a systematic sales' system with cold and warm outreach here on skool and jumping on sales' calls with the sales' script I developed.5-Calculated my Key Performance Indicators and reverse engineered them into daily outreach volume to hit my desired income.6- Recorded my calls and reiterated it in order to improve.7- After 3 months of no results but pure consistency I landed in one week my three first high ticket clients and surpassed $10K... (it took a while to get the sales' script and the correct outreach volume right)I think that sums it up...

author: Scott Northwolf
title: Don't digress. Be simple...
text:
Recently I was on a coaching call with one of my clients and at a certain point I was going rounds and rounds trying to break one of his limiting beliefs and he just said to me "Brother, be simple. Get to the point."
I could tell how tired he was of me just repeating myself every time we'd disagree and I realized then...
"Wait a minute! Why is this not working? Why is this taking so long? Why do I feel like I'm speaking for ages and getting nowhere?"
Then it struck me even harder when I was on two separate calls with other coaches while we were talking about business.
They'd digress like crazy and it'd feel like pure ADHD every step of the conversation. Then I realized they were a mirror to my own behaviour and I could tell how tiring it was the whole deal.
Since then I have decided to try to keep it simple.
Condensed the biggest amount of high leverage knowledge in the shortest sentences possible and focus on actionable steps way more than on overexplaining super complex concepts just to satisfy my own ego and show just how smart I am (I am, and also the most humble man in the universe in case you haven't been able to tell by now).
Being comfortable speaking less and even remaining in silence it's an art and it brings you more power, not less.
Striking the balance is essential.
Speak too much and you'll come across weak and as if you have something to proof.
We all have something to proof but we must start by proving that to ourselves so it comes off naturally and it's projected from a place of abundance, groundness and relaxation instead of from a place of lack and fear.
Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brother! Remeber: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

author: Thomas El Nadir
text:
Love this, man. I also have the exact same tendancy to go on long proses and tangents when all could be said in shorter, more impactful ways.
You also summed up the conclusion I would put to it : "Striking the balance is essential."
We have a depth and thoughtfulness superpower.
With great power comes great responsibilities, and it is ours to make our wisdom accessible and actionable for those that can (and want to) benefit from it, in the way that hits home for them.

author: Lea Newkirk
title: 🌒Synthesis Requires Silence.
text: We often think of rest as a "reward" for being productive (most times I still do). I am starting to realize that’s just bad math. In the high-intensity environment of self-improvement, where we are constantly upgrading our internal software, if I don’t schedule the off time, my nervous system just schedules a burnout for me. Rest isn't the opposite of productivity, it’s the essential cooldown phase that allows the next sprint to happen. I mean, we wouldn't run a high-end processor at 100% capacity 24/7, it would melt. Our biology is no different. I’m curious, in an environment like this, how do you personally distinguish between "healthy friction" and a nervous system that’s starting to redline, and what are the specific warning signs you usually miss until it’s too late?

author: Harrison Todd
text: Rest is productive (when done right).I'd say one of the ways to make sure you're not just resting to procrastinate work, is by only resting when you planned to rest.When you have time set aside to rest should probably change over time (at least for a bit) while you figure out when you actually need rest and when it works best to take a break for you.I'd say it just takes trial and error though.

author: Scott Northwolf
text: @Harrison Todd this is actually a VERY powerful insight...if you schedule time to rest on advance you know for a fact you are not procrastinating but being disciplined enough to rest periodically and systematically.

author: Lea Newkirk
title: Everyday is the best day ever! 🥹
text:
A while ago, I learnt the concept of confirmation bias. Basically, the human tendency to only pay attention to information that proves what we already believe, while ignoring anything that doesn't. The way I act on this is, waking up, deciding it is already the best day ever and my job is to hunt for all the reasons why (at least 3), within minutes I already have so many reasons and I have not had a bad day since 😂 not because bad things don't happen (that's unrealistic) but because regardless, whatever I focus on will confirm what I already believe.
I am very curious, if anyone else does something similar, please share! I would love to hear about your experience and what usually confirms that the day will be an amazing one?
